failure_condition: Any corpus where partitions differ or spectra diverge beyond 1e-12.
hypothesis: The v2 self-contained kernel is behaviorally equivalent to the v1 kernel: identical partitions, VI values, and merge spectra on the v1 fixture corpora.
interpretation_limits: Equivalence claim applies only to tested corpora and the ported function set; it does not certify performance, API compatibility, or behavior on unseen geometries.
success_condition: VI(P_v1, P_v2) == 0 and merge spectra equal to 1e-12 on all fixture corpora.

failure_condition: No encoder family exceeds the null on the declared repositories — extending the identity-degeneracy result from traces to code.
hypothesis: At least one independent encoder family's partition blocks predict co-change coupling (commits touching multiple entities) significantly better than size-matched random partitions, across >=20 repositories.
interpretation_limits: Positive or negative, the result applies only to the declared repositories, encoder families, and ground-truth signals. Scores are never pooled across families. Cyclomatic complexity is EXCLUDED as ground truth (it is computed from the same artifact the encoders measure: circular). Primary ground truth: co-change coupling from git history. Secondary: human-labeled module boundaries. No claim about untested codebases.
success_condition: One or more families: block co-residency predicts co-change with effect size exceeding the 95th percentile of the random-partition null, on >=60% of declared repositories.
